snippet: Designated teachers are nominated by the school principal, and are expected to hold a relatively senior position within the school. Each LOOKOUT Education Support Centre External Link provides training for designated teachers in their region to be the advocate and champion for students at their school who are in out-of-home care. Designated teachers are given guidance on the needs of students in out-of-home care and strategies for supporting the education of these students. Designated teachers are the main point of contact between the school and LOOKOUT Centre staff. The designated teacher has a key role in supporting students in out-of-home care to make a smooth transition into school, including making sure there are effective arrangements in place for the speedy transfer of information between relevant agencies. In conjunction with LOOKOUT Centres, the designated teacher ensures students are engaged in education and meeting their learning goals. They work collaboratively with the school’s wellbeing staff and other agency representatives to minimise any disruption to the student’s education.
